title: Beauticians and related occupations
description: All artists and related workers not listed separately. Play prerecorded music for live audiences at venues or events such as clubs, parties, or wedding receptions. May use techniques such as mixing, cutting, or sampling to manipulate recordings. May also perform as emcee (master of ceremonies). All entertainers and performers, sports and related workers not listed separately. (Based on 17 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Stay informed of the latest styles and hair care techniques and trends.
Cut, trim, and shape hair or hairpieces according to clients' instructions, hair type, and facial features, using scissors, clippers, and razors.
Drape and pin protective cloths around customers' shoulders and maintain clean workstations.
Question patrons regarding desired services, hairstyles, and offer treatment suggestions to alleviate hair problems.
Shampoo and condition hair and scalp, including performing scalp massages to clean and remove excess oil.
Provide skin care and nail treatments, such as facials, manicures, and massages using specialized compounds, products, and techniques.
Apply, recommend, and sell cosmetics, lotions, and skin-care products including advising clients on their use and benefits.
Shape, trim, and shave beards and moustaches, using scissors, razors, and other grooming tools.
Evaluate clients' skin and nails, performing necessary treatments to improve their condition and resilience.
Record services provided and schedule appointments, while managing payments and maintaining treatment records.
Apply makeup to enhance or alter appearance for performances, including selecting shades and maintaining continuity throughout productions.
Clean, sanitize, and maintain tools and work environments, ensuring safe and hygienic conditions for clients.
Participate in continuing education to stay abreast of industry trends and developments.
Meet with managers or other supervisors to stay informed of changes affecting operations.
Observe and evaluate workers' appearance and performance to ensure quality service and compliance with specifications.
Resolve customer complaints regarding worker performance or services rendered.
Recruit and hire staff members.
Train workers in proper operational procedures and functions and explain company policies.
Assign work schedules, following work requirements, to ensure quality and timely delivery of service.
Direct or coordinate the activities of entertainment and recreation related workers.
Inspect work areas or operating equipment to ensure conformance to established standards in areas such as cleanliness or maintenance.
Enforce safety rules and regulations.

emerging_tasks.task: Assist hair stylists with chemical services, such as neutralizing perms and applying hair color.
Clean and sanitize supplies, such as makeup brushes.
Launder and fold the towels that are used for drying customers' hair.
Refill and stock work stations with supplies, such as shampoos and conditioners.
Rinse out hair color or permanent solutions from customers' hair.
Sweep hair from the salon floor.
Create patterns for costumes based on designer's drawings.
